     The Numbers Business Report

Home Again (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $27,020,284Details
International Box Office $10,316,590
Worldwide Box Office $37,336,874
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Alice Kinney is recently separated from her husband. Alice decides to start over by moving back to her hometown of Los Angeles with her two young daughters. During a night out on her 40th birthday, Alice meets three aspiring filmmakers who happen to be in need of a place to live. Alice agrees to let the guys stay in her guest house temporarily, but the arrangement ends up unfolding in unexpected ways. Alice’s unlikely new family and new romance comes to a crashing halt when her ex-husband shows up, suitcase in hand.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$8,567,881 (31.7% of total gross)
Legs:3.15 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:72.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$15,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,940 opening theaters/3,036 max. theaters, 4.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $36,027,048

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 8th, 2017 (Wide) by Open Road
International Releases: September 8th, 2017 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
September 8th, 2017 (Wide), released as Mi Nueva Yo (Mexico)
September 15th, 2017 (Wide) (Czech Republic)
September 15th, 2017 (Wide) (Slovakia)
September 22nd, 2017 (Wide), released as Uma Casa Cheia (Portugal)
Video Release: November 21st, 2017 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some thematic and sexual material.
(Rating bulletin 2484 (Cert #51107), 7/12/2017)
Running Time: 97 minutes
Geographical setting: Los Angeles
Plot point: Adult Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent, Movie Director, Relationships Gone Wrong, Romance, Single Parent
Social setting: Movie Business
Subgenre: Romantic Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Romantic Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Open Road Films, Waverly Films, Black Bicycle
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Alice Kinney is recently separated from her husband. Alice decides to start over by moving back to her hometown of Los Angeles with her two young daughters. During a night out on her 40th birthday, Alice meets three aspiring filmmakers who happen to be in need of a place to live. Alice agrees to let the guys stay in her guest house temporarily, but the arrangement ends up unfolding in unexpected ways. Alice’s unlikely new family and new romance comes to a crashing halt when her ex-husband shows up, suitcase in hand.
